title: One who does not lust for forms, Seeing a form, does not cling, His mind does not adhere to the feeling, He does not remain…
content:
One who does not lust for forms,
Seeing a form, does not cling,
His mind does not adhere to the feeling,
He does not remain attached to forms.
As he sees forms,
Feeling feelings accordingly,
It wanes away, not accumulated —
Thus mindful he practices.
Thus suffering is not stored up,
He is said to be close to Nibbāna.

One who does not lust for sounds,
Hearing a sound, does not cling …
One who does not lust for odors,
Smelling an odor, does not cling …
One who does not lust for tastes,
Tasting a flavor, does not cling …
One who does not lust for touches,
Feeling a touch, does not cling …

title: “Monks, if there were no gratification in the world, beings would not become lustful for the world. But because there is gratification in the world,…
content: “Monks, if there were no gratification in the world, beings would not become lustful for the world. But because there is gratification in the world, beings become lustful for the world. If there were no peril in the world, beings would not become disenchanted with the world. But because there is peril in the world, beings become disenchanted with the world. If there were no escape from the world, beings would not escape from the world. But because there is escape from the world, beings escape from the world.”

title: Elder Rādha: As rain seeps into a house with a faulty roof, So lust seeps into an undeveloped mind. As rain does not seep into…
content:
Elder Rādha:

As rain seeps into a house with a faulty roof,
So lust seeps into an undeveloped mind.

As rain does not seep into a house with a good roof,
So lust does not seep into a well-developed mind.

title: “Here, venerable sir, a person has a stain and he truly knows: ‘There is a stain in me.’ For such a person, it can be…
content: “Here, venerable sir, a person has a stain and he truly knows: ‘There is a stain in me.’ For such a person, it can be expected: ‘He will arouse desire, make effort, arouse energy to abandon that stain. He will die without greed, without ill-will, without delusion, without stain, his mind unpolluted.’ Venerable sir, just as a bronze bowl brought from the market or from the smithy, covered with dust and grime, and the owner uses it, wipes it, and does not throw it into a dusty place – that bowl, after a time, becomes more purified and cleaner.”

title: Greed, hatred, delusion — these three, Are evil minds for a person, They damage one’s own self, They produce one’s own self, Like the rind…
content:
Greed, hatred, delusion — these three,
Are evil minds for a person,
They damage one’s own self,
They produce one’s own self,
Like the rind and core of a tree,
Producing its own fruit.

title: “Monks, such is the peril in the case of forms. Furthermore, monks, one sees that woman’s body thrown in the charnel ground, just a skeleton…
content:
“Monks, such is the peril in the case of forms. Furthermore, monks, one sees that woman’s body thrown in the charnel ground, just a skeleton with white bones like a shell … bones more than a year old … rotting bones reduced to powder. What do you think, monks? Has the former charm and beauty disappeared and peril become evident?”
“Yes, venerable sir.”
“Monks, such is the peril in the case of forms.”

title: “Monks, if one does not intend, does not plan, but still has a tendency towards something, that becomes a basis for the establishment of consciousness.…
content: “Monks, if one does not intend, does not plan, but still has a tendency towards something, that becomes a basis for the establishment of consciousness. When there is a basis, there is an establishment of consciousness. When consciousness is established and grows, there is a production of future renewed existence. When there is future renewed existence, future birth, aging, death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief, and despair arise. Such is the origin of this whole mass of suffering.”

title: The nun Vijayā, thinking “This is Māra,” recited this verse to Māra: Forms, sounds, tastes, smells, contacts — They delude the mind — I return…
content:
The nun Vijayā, thinking “This is Māra,” recited this verse to Māra:

Forms, sounds, tastes, smells, contacts —
They delude the mind —
I return them to you, Māra —
I have no need for them.
Regarding this impure body,
Easily broken and fragile,
I am humiliated and ashamed —
Craving for sensual desire is destroyed.
Those reborn in the form realm,
Those aspiring to the formless realm,
And those who attain peaceful concentration —
Everywhere, darkness is destroyed.

title: “And craving, previously unknowing — which is of sensual desire, of defilement — that state is abandoned, cut off at the root, made like a…
content: “And craving, previously unknowing — which is of sensual desire, of defilement — that state is abandoned, cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, unable to arise again in the future. And anger, previously unknowing — which is of ill-will, of fault — that state is abandoned, cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, unable to arise again in the future. And ignorance, previously unknowing — which is of delusion, of fault — that state is abandoned, cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, unable to arise again in the future. Therefore, a monk thus attained has attained the supreme peace of the peaceful state. Monks, this is the supreme noble peace — namely, the peace of greed, hatred, and delusion.”

title: “Monks, there are these five strands of sensual pleasure. What five? Forms cognizable by the eye — desirable, agreeable, pleasant, charming, connected with sensuality, enticing;…
content: “Monks, there are these five strands of sensual pleasure. What five? Forms cognizable by the eye — desirable, agreeable, pleasant, charming, connected with sensuality, enticing; sounds cognizable by the ear … odors … tastes … touches cognizable by the body — desirable … enticing. These, monks, are the five strands of sensual pleasure. To abandon these five strands of sensual pleasure, monks, the four foundations of mindfulness should be developed.”
